item.title: V2 Retail Ltd Gains 3.39%: 4 Key Factors Driving This Week’s Market Moves
item.description: V2 Retail Ltd recorded a 3.39% gain over the week ending 27 March 2026, closing at Rs.197.70 compared to Rs.191.22 the previous Friday. This performance notably outpaced the Sensex, which declined 1.46% during the same period, reflecting a week of mixed technical signals, valuation reassessments, and strong intraday volatility for the small-cap garment and apparel company. Time: 52 minutes ago

item.title: Capri Global Capital Ltd’s Volatile Week: -2.74% Amid Mixed Technical Signals
item.description: Capri Global Capital Ltd experienced a turbulent week ending 27 March 2026, with its stock price declining 2.74% from Rs.170.00 to Rs.165.35, slightly underperforming the Sensex which fell 1.46% over the same period. The week was marked by sharp intraday swings, sectoral pressures in the NBFC space, and mixed technical signals that together shaped the stock’s volatile performance. Time: 52 minutes ago

item.title: Zenith Steel Pipes & Industries Ltd Falls 4.80%: 3 Key Events Shaping the Week
item.description: Zenith Steel Pipes & Industries Ltd experienced a turbulent week from 23 to 27 March 2026, closing down 4.80% at Rs.4.76 compared to a 1.46% decline in the Sensex. The stock saw sharp swings, hitting both lower and upper circuit limits amid heavy selling and buying pressures, reflecting heightened volatility and investor uncertainty in this micro-cap iron and steel products company. Time: 52 minutes ago

item.title: L&T Finance Ltd Falls 3.56%: 3 Key Factors Driving This Week’s Volatility
item.description: L&T Finance Ltd experienced a turbulent week ending 27 March 2026, with its stock price declining by 3.56% to close at Rs.251.75, underperforming the Sensex which fell 1.46%. The week was marked by sharp intraday price pressure, a notable shift in valuation metrics prompting a downgrade to Hold, and a surge in derivatives open interest signalling mixed market sentiment amid sectoral volatility. Time: 52 minutes ago

item.title: Transport Corporation of India Ltd’s Volatile Week: -0.64% Amid Market Swings
item.description: Transport Corporation of India Ltd (TCI) experienced a volatile week from 23 to 27 March 2026, closing marginally lower by 0.64% at Rs.941.45 despite a strong midweek rebound. The stock underperformed the Sensex, which declined 1.46% over the same period. Key events included a sharp fall to a 52-week low on 23 March followed by a significant gap up on 24 March, reflecting mixed investor sentiment amid broader market weakness. Time: 52 minutes ago

item.title: Asian Granito India Ltd Falls 2.57%: Downgrade and Valuation Shifts Shape Weekly Trend
item.description: Asian Granito India Ltd’s stock declined by 2.57% over the week ending 27 March 2026, closing at Rs.58.81 against a Sensex fall of 1.46%. The week was marked by a significant downgrade from MarketsMOJO to a Sell rating amid mixed financial and technical signals, alongside a recalibration of valuation metrics from very attractive to attractive. Despite a strong quarterly performance, the stock faced selling pressure, reflecting investor caution amid deteriorating technical momentum and subdued long-term fundamentals. Time: 52 minutes ago

item.title: Thermax Ltd. Declines 1.04% Despite Mixed Technical Signals and Market Volatility
item.description: Thermax Ltd. closed the week marginally lower by 1.04% at Rs.3,192.35, slightly outperforming the Sensex which fell 1.46%. The stock experienced a volatile week marked by a sharp decline on 23 March, followed by a strong rebound on 24 March, and a mixed technical momentum emerging towards the week’s end. Despite positive intraday gains midweek, the overall sentiment remains cautious amid mixed indicator signals and a recent downgrade to a Sell rating by MarketsMOJO. Time: 53 minutes ago

item.title: V R Woodart Ltd Gains 9.97%: 5 Key Factors Driving the Rally
item.description: V R Woodart Ltd delivered a strong weekly performance, gaining 9.97% from Rs.106.92 to Rs.117.58 between 23 and 27 March 2026, significantly outperforming the Sensex which declined 1.46% over the same period. The stock hit multiple new 52-week and all-time highs during the week, reflecting robust momentum amid a volatile broader market environment. Time: 53 minutes ago

item.title: Shyam Century Ferrous Ltd Falls 14.67%: 6 Key Factors Driving the Sharp Decline
item.description: Shyam Century Ferrous Ltd’s stock endured a challenging week from 23 to 27 March 2026, plunging 14.67% to close at Rs.3.84, significantly underperforming the Sensex’s modest 1.46% decline. The stock hit multiple 52-week lows amid heavy selling pressure, technical breakdowns, and deteriorating financial fundamentals, reflecting persistent headwinds for this micro-cap ferrous metals company. Time: 53 minutes ago

item.title: Rajapalayam Mills Ltd’s Subdued Week: -0.93% Price Change Amid Valuation Shift
item.description: Rajapalayam Mills Ltd’s stock closed the week marginally lower by 0.93% at Rs.736.30, slightly outperforming the Sensex which fell 1.46%. The week was marked by fresh 52-week lows, a downgrade to a strong sell rating, and a shift in valuation metrics, all unfolding amid volatile market conditions and mixed financial signals. Time: 53 minutes ago

item.title: Ambuja Cements Ltd’s Volatile Week: -2.79% Amid Mixed Market Signals and Technical Pressure
item.description: Ambuja Cements Ltd experienced a turbulent week from 23 to 27 March 2026, with its share price declining 2.79% to close at Rs.408.95, underperforming the Sensex’s 1.46% fall. The stock hit a fresh 52-week low early in the week before staging short-lived rebounds midweek, only to retreat again amid renewed selling pressure. This review analyses the key events and market dynamics shaping Ambuja’s price action over the week. Time: 53 minutes ago

item.title: Amba Enterprises Ltd Drops 2.48%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Week’s Volatility
item.description: Amba Enterprises Ltd’s stock closed the week at Rs.104.35, down 2.48% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.107.00, underperforming the Sensex which declined 1.46% over the same period. The week was marked by sharp intraday swings, including a significant rebound on 24 March followed by renewed selling pressure, culminating in a fresh 52-week low on 27 March. These movements reflected a combination of broader market weakness, sector-specific challenges, and shifting valuation perceptions. Time: 53 minutes ago

item.title: Omkar Speciality Chemicals Ltd Drops 5.51%: 4 Key Events Shaping the Week
item.description: Omkar Speciality Chemicals Ltd experienced a turbulent week from 23 to 27 March 2026, closing down 5.51% at Rs.3.77, underperforming the Sensex which declined 1.46%. The stock saw sharp swings, hitting upper circuit limits twice early in the week before succumbing to heavy selling pressure and a new 52-week low by Friday. Despite brief bursts of strong buying interest, the overall sentiment turned bearish amid weak fundamentals and limited liquidity. Time: 53 minutes ago

item.title: Sejal Glass Ltd Falls 7.26%: Valuation Shift and Lower Circuit Highlight Volatility
item.description: Sejal Glass Ltd experienced a turbulent week ending 27 Mar 2026, with its share price declining 7.26% to close at Rs.446.10, significantly underperforming the Sensex’s 1.46% fall. The stock’s week was marked by an initial valuation upgrade amid mixed market conditions, followed by intense selling pressure culminating in a lower circuit hit on the final trading day. These developments underscore the stock’s heightened volatility and shifting investor sentiment within a challenging market environment. Time: 53 minutes ago

item.title: NIBE Ltd Falls 9.10% This Week: 3 Key Factors Behind the Continued Downtrend
item.description: NIBE Ltd’s shares declined sharply over the week ending 27 March 2026, falling 9.10% from Rs.957.65 to Rs.870.50, significantly underperforming the Sensex’s 1.46% drop. The stock hit multiple 52-week lows amid persistent financial challenges, bearish technical indicators, and a weak broader market environment. Despite intermittent gains midweek, the overall trend remained negative, reflecting ongoing pressure on the aerospace and defence company’s valuation and outlook. Time: 53 minutes ago

item.title: PCBL Chemical Ltd Gains 2.82%: 4 Key Factors Driving the Week’s Volatility
item.description: PCBL Chemical Ltd experienced a turbulent week from 23 to 27 March 2026, ultimately closing with a modest gain of 2.82% at Rs.255.30, outperforming the Sensex which declined by 1.46% over the same period. The stock’s journey was marked by sharp intraday swings, a fresh 52-week low early in the week, followed by a strong rebound driven by heightened trading volumes and institutional interest. This review analyses the key events shaping the stock’s performance and the broader market context. Time: 53 minutes ago

item.title: Inox Wind Ltd Falls 3.14% Amid Volatile Week: 4 Key Market Signals
item.description: Inox Wind Ltd experienced a turbulent week ending 27 March 2026, with its stock price declining 3.14% to close at Rs.79.25, underperforming the Sensex’s 1.46% fall. The week was marked by a new 52-week low, valuation shifts, and heightened derivatives activity, reflecting a complex interplay of sectoral pressures and market sentiment. Time: 53 minutes ago

item.title: Zydus Wellness Ltd Gains 5.01%: 2 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Momentum
item.description: Zydus Wellness Ltd delivered a notable weekly gain of 5.01%, closing at Rs.446.05 on 27 March 2026, outperforming the Sensex which declined by 1.46% over the same period. The stock’s recovery was driven by a strong intraday surge on 24 March and a technical momentum shift on 27 March, reflecting a complex but cautiously optimistic outlook amid mixed market signals. Time: 53 minutes ago

item.title: Prestige Estates Projects Ltd Falls 5.98%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Decline
item.description: Prestige Estates Projects Ltd experienced a turbulent week, closing at Rs.1,172.70 on 27 March 2026, down 5.98% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.1,247.30. This decline notably outpaced the Sensex’s 1.46% fall over the same period, reflecting heightened volatility amid bearish market conditions and increased derivatives activity signalling shifting investor sentiment. Time: 53 minutes ago

item.title: Schaeffler India Ltd Falls 4.66%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Decline
item.description: Schaeffler India Ltd’s shares declined by 4.66% over the week ending 27 March 2026, closing at Rs.3,985.40 compared to Rs.4,180.40 the previous Friday. This underperformance contrasted with the BSE Sensex’s smaller 1.46% fall, signalling a week marked by significant volatility and sectoral headwinds amid mixed technical signals and an upgrade to a Hold rating. Time: 54 minutes ago
